distinctive_features: rolls along the underside of an inversion layer
visual_attributes.texture: smooth and continuous at the leading edge, turbulent at the rear
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: up to 1,000 kilometers long, 1 to 2 kilometers high
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: series of consecutive roll clouds, sometimes up to ten

domain_specific_visual.cloud_formation: formed at the leading edge, eroded at the trailing edge

domain_specific_functional.weather_impact: can lead to showers or thunderstorms

relationships.description: The only known location where it can be predicted and observed regularly.

contextual_attributes.historical_period: First reported by Royal Australian Air Force in 1942
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Primarily Gulf of Carpentaria, occasionally observed globally
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Known as kangólgi by the local Garrwa Aboriginal people
